{
  "analysis": "The autotune process appears stuck after training completed (no evaluation/save output for a while), and the best autotune score was only 0.5959, below the 0.62 target. I should interrupt this and try a more targeted approach: train with more epochs for better accuracy, then use fastText quantization to reduce model size under 150MB.",
  "plan": "I will interrupt the stuck process, then train a model with more epochs (25) and similar hyperparameters, evaluate it, and then quantize the model to reduce size. Quantization should significantly reduce the model size while preserving accuracy.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "C-c",
      "duration": 0.5
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 - <<'PY'
import fasttext, time, os

t0 = time.time()
model = fasttext.train_supervised(
    input='/app/train.ft',
    epoch=25,
    lr=0.5,
    wordNgrams=2,
    dim=100,
    bucket=200000,
    minCount=2,
    loss='softmax',
    thread=4,
    verbose=2,
)
print('train time', time.time()-t0)

n, p, r = model.test('/app/test.ft')
print('unquantized test lines', n, 'precision', p, 'recall', r)

model.save_model('/app/model_unquantized.bin')
print('unquantized model size MB', os.path.getsize('/app/model_unquantized.bin')/(1024*1024))

# Quantize the model to reduce size
model.quantize(input='/app/train.ft', retrain=True, verbose=2)
n, p, r = model.test('/app/test.ft')
print('quantized test lines', n, 'precision', p, 'recall', r)

model.save_model('/app/model.bin')
print('quantized model size MB', os.path.getsize('/app/model.bin')/(1024*1024))
PY
",
      "duration": 60.0
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": false
}
